fact: March 12 sits exactly 9 days before the spring equinox, making it the perfect 'almost spring' day when winter starts losing its grip!
In the Northern Hemisphere, daylight is growing by about 3 minutes every single day around March 12 - that's like gaining an entire hour of sunlight every 20 days!
Skunks typically wake up from their winter sleepiness around this time and start wandering around looking for mates, which means way more stinky encounters for everyone!
Tree sap starts flowing like crazy in maple trees around March 12 in cold regions - some trees can produce 10 gallons of sap in a single day!
Tree pollen often explodes into the air around this date, creating invisible yellow clouds that make many people sneeze!
March 12 falls smack in the middle of 'mud season' in northern climates, when frozen ground turns into the sloppiest, stickiest, boot-stealing mud imaginable!
Earthworms start wiggling up to the surface around this time because the soil is finally warm enough for them to move - some can stretch 14 inches long!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets almost exactly due west around March 12, making it super easy to figure out directions without a compass!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 12 marks the start of serious autumn weather, with leaves turning brilliant reds and oranges!
Chipmunks typically emerge from their underground burrows around this date after sleeping for months, and they're absolutely starving - they can eat 15 nuts in just a few minutes!
March 12 lands right in the peak of 'sugar season' when maple syrup makers work around the clock because the perfect freeze-thaw cycle only lasts a few weeks!
The zodiac switches from Pisces to Aries just 8 days after March 12, making this date part of the dreamy, imaginative fish sign's final splash!
